text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 1
Executive summary

paragraphs.text: New market entry for India recruiting: flagship narrative is Know Your Candidate plus high-volume automation; Teleperformance India (P1–P5) and five internal SMEs show trust and throughput breaking without lawful ID gates and bulk intelligence.
DPDP, non-Aadhaar-first private hiring after the 2018 Section 57 limits, plus EU GDPR and AI Act rails, shape matching, screening, and notices for multinational tenants hosting India pipelines.

Competitive scan shows true gaps on +91 SMS, WhatsApp in core UI, Naukri-class native multipost, and UIDAI eKYC; native strengths are UDMF, bulk grid, Hindi, consent or retention levers, and BGV business process plus Core Connector patterns.

speaker_notes:
• Stress lawful identifiers versus customer language on Aadhaar matching.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Priority moves: government-ID-aware Know Your Candidate with partner eKYC; scalable duplicate and agency throughput; governed HiredScore for India volume templates; native WhatsApp and +91 when roadmap allows; offer and EA agility at batch scale.
Sequence channel investments with honest partner maps on boards and messaging until native parity closes RFP gaps without over-claiming government eKYC that private employers cannot mandate.

Customer ideation quant (global TA filter) corroborates hardest pain in Communications, Offers, Candidates, and Job Requisitions, aligning with Teleperformance India interviews despite sparse India-named strings in the export.

speaker_notes:
• Part two keeps executive synthesis within density limits.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 2
Research challenge

paragraphs.text: How should Workday Recruiting win India enterprise and high-volume programmes on trust, compliance, and recruiter throughput while staying honest on channels and boards?
Objectives: validate identity and dedupe economics, offer and BGC friction, governance off-platform, channel reality, and observability versus suite strengths and configurable privacy levers.

Evidence: strategy context, PESTEL, SWOT, India competitive matrix and scan, SMEs (SME1–SME5), customers (P1–P5), global ideation quant (about ten thousand TA-filtered ideas), thematic synthesis with cross-source matrix.

speaker_notes:
• Avoid claiming presales row counts.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Scope is India-hosted recruiting journeys for enterprise and BPO-scale programmes with Know Your Candidate and high-volume lenses from April 2026 strategy context.

Presales export yields three India-keyword-matched Talent Acquisition rows (thin versus full territory coverage); Win/Loss pairs those rows with ideation volume, matrix true gaps, and Teleperformance India interviews.
Full thematic write-up, PESTEL, SWOT, and eleven ranked roadmap rows live in the April 2026 India analysis markdown for detail beyond this deck.

speaker_notes:
• Transparent boundary for leadership.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 3
Context review

paragraphs.text: India is a named Q2 scale-growth row: eight customer wins, DPDP-led compliance programmes, and local job boards through certified partner distribution rather than native board builds (strategy context, April 2026).
Corporate Priority 1 remains GCC readiness; India work must still clear the same bar on privacy rigour and honest channel maps without assuming GCC-only resourcing.
Know Your Candidate and high-volume hiring are explicit mission lenses; buyers pair India growth with GDPR, EU AI Act, and DPDP diligence on consent, transfers, and automated shortlisting.

speaker_notes:
• India row first for this deck audience.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: IMARC sizes India HR technology at approximately USD 1,210 million in 2025 toward USD 2,440 million by 2034 at 7.71% CAGR (2026–2034), citing ATS, social recruiting, cloud, AI, and mobile drivers (January 2026).

GCC and IT services narratives cite lakhs of annual job creation and fresher intake recovery, reinforcing campaign-style throughput as the norm for enterprise talent acquisition (trade press 2025–2026).

DataReportal Digital 2026 India reports about 1.03 billion internet users (70.0% penetration), 1.06 billion mobile connections (72.5% of population), and 170 million LinkedIn members, anchoring mobile-first candidate journeys (October 2025 data).

speaker_notes:
• Figures are directional; reconcile definitions in-room.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 4
Product strategy

paragraphs.text: India first: medium regional priority with eight Q2 wins, DPDP programmes, and local boards via Broadbean-class partners per April 2026 strategy context (no native board builds).
Priority 2 AI matching: activate HiredScore for high-volume tenants with human review, logging, and explainability for EU AI Act and DPDP significant-fiduciary narratives when applicable.
Priority 3 core ATS parity plus GCC Priority 1 pattern: bulk actions, mobile recruiter, BGV integrations, Paradox where licensed; India matches proof on consent, retention, and partner-led boards.

speaker_notes:
• Map each bullet to OKRs in quarterly markdown when challenged.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: India: medium priority; eight customers; DPDP; local job boards; scale-growth narrative for this readout.
GCC: high priority; ten wins; WhatsApp and SMS; nationalisation; Arabic RTL; boards via Broadbean-class partners.
Japan: medium; five expansions; two-step offer; APPI; LINE.
Australia: medium; three expansions; Fair Work; SEEK via Broadbean.

speaker_notes:
• Focal region appears first for non-GCC regional decks per style guide.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Suite depth: HCM, Recruiting, Talent, and Learning on one record versus point ATS or payroll-first bundles.
AI-powered: HiredScore and Paradox when activated versus manual high-volume screening and scheduling.
Compliance-first: GDPR, EU AI Act, and global privacy posture with DPDP-ready consent, retention, purge, and audit trails.
Vulnerabilities: niche board coverage via partners, mobile recruiter catch-up, scheduling activation not universal; message honestly on native SMS, WhatsApp, Naukri UX, and Aadhaar eKYC gaps.

speaker_notes:
• Tie to SWOT and matrix true-gap rows.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 5
PESTEL

paragraphs.text: Labour law consolidation toward four codes with phased 2026 commentary reshapes contracts, fixed-term parity, and social security expectations buyers must mirror from hire to HCM (Bar and Bench 2026; labour.gov.in IRC 2020).
Code on Social Security 2020 and gig narratives (DD News; IANS) widen non-traditional employment types sourcing and onboarding must cover beside permanent hires.
PLI press late 2025 cites Rs 2.16 lakh crore investment and about 14.39 lakh jobs, sustaining bulk hiring; MeitY sovereign cloud talk raises in-country hosting expectations (PIB; TechCircle 2026).

text.text: Treat India as policy-active: flexible hiring metadata into HCM transitions, throughput for PLI and GCC-style ramps, India hosting options where sovereignty influences RFPs, without one-size employment categories.

speaker_notes:
• Cite labour.gov.in for statutory anchors.

References:
• https://labour.gov.in/acts/industrial-relations-code-2020-31-dec-2020
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: IMARC sizes India HR technology near USD 1,210M (2025) toward USD 2,440M (2034) at 7.71% CAGR; ATS, social recruiting, cloud, AI, and mobile are named growth drivers (January 2026).
IT fresher and GCC hiring press (SightsInPlus; GCC RISE) frames sustained enterprise throughput rather than episodic hiring, supporting capital allocation to scale tooling.
Workday AWS India data centre and team expansion messaging signals vendor commitment and placement flexibility that matters in data-residency conversations (newsroom July 2025).

text.text: Scale justifies India-first throughput: queue-safe bulk actions, identity-aware dedupe, operational analytics; local hosting supports deals where residency gates contracts.

speaker_notes:
• IMARC anchors sizing.

References:
• https://www.imarcgroup.com/human-resource-technology-market-india

paragraphs.text: DataReportal Digital 2026 India: about 1.03B internet users (70.0%), 1.06B mobile connections (72.5%), 500M social identities; mobile-first apply and messaging are baseline (October 2025).
About 170M LinkedIn members in India per ad tools in the same report; consumer platforms fragment while the professional graph stays concentrated for sourcing.
WhatsApp is default business messaging in practice literature; open-rate comparisons to email should be framed as directional vendor guidance, not official statistics.

text.text: Design mobile-first apply, SMS and WhatsApp-class partner channels for scheduling, LinkedIn-heavy sourcing, templated local comms, and explicit first-contact consent.

speaker_notes:
• Pair channels with DPDP consent.

References:
• https://www.datareportal.com/reports/digital-2026-india

paragraphs.text: Deloitte India March 2026: ~40% Indian respondents report significant or full AI usage versus ~28% global; product, strategy, ops, and marketing lead at-scale adoption.
~68% prioritise security and compliance for AI scale; ~39% cite regulatory integration as top challenge versus speed-only narratives.
ICRIER-linked press (Feb 2026) notes AI shifting junior hiring while hybrid skills rise; DC and sovereign AI infra stories support in-country latency expectations.

text.text: Activate HiredScore and scheduling where demanded; pair with audit logs, human review, explainable workflows, and DPDP notices for automated shortlisting under fiduciary scrutiny.

speaker_notes:
• Deloitte press primary anchor.

References:
• https://www.deloitte.com/in/en/about/press-room/indian-enterprises-lead-global-peers-in-at-scale-ai-adoption-across-most-functions.html

paragraphs.text: No recruiting-specific environmental statute; listed employers still face SEBI BRSR workforce and inclusion disclosure pressure that hiring metrics can support (filing season 2025).
BRSR nudges reportable diversity metrics upstream from payroll; green-jobs press shifts sector skill mixes more than it forces ATS compliance features today.
DATA GAP: weak authoritative link from environmental regulation to recruiting SKU scope; treat sustainability modules as watch-list, not a FY27 ATS build priority.

text.text: Stay secondary on environmental SKUs; enable diversity metrics from hiring into BRSR narratives; monitor green-skills taxonomies for packs not heavy sustainability builds.

speaker_notes:
• Reporting enabler positioning.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: DPDP Act 2023: fiduciaries and principals, consent and duties, breach notification, Board enforcement; Rules 2025 cover notices, children, grievances, transfers (Mondaq summaries).
Rule 15 cross-border commentary often cites May 2027 planning horizon; confirm dates with counsel before commitments.
SC 2018 kept Aadhaar core but struck Section 57 for private mandatory use; EU GDPR and AI Act Annex III still bind multinationals on transfers, DPIAs, and high-risk hiring AI.

text.text: Ship granular consent and notices, processor governance, retention and deletion for DPDP, lawful non-Aadhaar-first identity; preserve GDPR rights tooling and human oversight for EU-affected processing.

speaker_notes:
• Not legal advice.

References:
• https://www.mondaq.com/india/privacy-protection/1406850/the-digital-personal-data-protection-act-2023-key-implications-for-employers
• https://gdpr-info.eu/

True gaps: native +91 SMS; native WhatsApp in core Recruiting UI; native UIDAI Aadhaar eKYC; OOTB Naukri-class multipost without multipartner or Studio; mobile recruiter partial parity; advanced semantic AI behind licence boundaries
Eight-customer India row; IMARC HR tech expansion; DPDP phased readiness as trust differentiator; HiredScore activation for high volume; partner-led boards and CPaaS runbooks
Regional suite TCO bundles; SAP, Oracle, iCIMS 2026 AI and omnichannel press; DPDP penalties and SDF duties; fraud discourse raising KYC demo bars without lawful Aadhaar defaults

• Source is Step 3 SWOT file, not thematic report.

References:
• research/India/swot-analysis-India-2026-04-01-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 7
Win / Loss

paragraphs.text: Opportunity Detail export in global gap-data folder, Talent Acquisition and five-year filter: 379 rows; India keyword scan finds three North America-segment deals citing India hiring in text fields.

Severity: none at levels 1-2; two at level 4 (scheduling across India or US hours; Candidate Home versus local India); one at level 5 (questionnaire agility on India reqs; buyers cite Greenhouse or iCIMS contrast).
No Aadhaar or fraud-primary rows appear in this filter; presales data underweights in-territory India, so pair these rows with interviews, ideation quant, and competitive matrix before roadmap bets.

speaker_notes:
• Ground counts in 108 gap analysis markdown.

References:
• research/India/gap-analysis/2026-04-01-gap-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

charts.title: Global TA ideation volume by capability (approx.)

paragraphs.text: About ten thousand Talent Acquisition-filtered ideas (export 30 March 2026) show Communications, Job Requisitions, Offers, and Candidates in the hardest sentiment-and-effort quadrant alongside Applications.
India-named strings were not found in the flattened export; treat counts as global reinforcement of capability buckets that interviews already stress.
Use this chart alongside the three India-linked presales rows and customer interviews so volume pain is visible even when CRM geography facets are incomplete.

speaker_notes:
• Values from 106 theme validation matrix.

References:
• research/India/brainstorm-analysis/2026-04-01-brainstorm-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

rows: India hours versus US time zones; partner-heavy workaround
~50% India hires off local site; account friction versus Candidate Home
High-volume India reqs; TA cannot adjust knock-outs vs HRIS locks
See SWOT plus in-competitive-matrix.md v1.11 for native versus workaround

speaker_notes:
• Buyer-reported severities; validate parity claims with competitive matrix.

References:
• research/India/gap-analysis/2026-04-01-gap-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md
• research/competitive/matrices/in-competitive-matrix.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 8
Ideation hub

paragraphs.text: P&T Idea Results Dashboard export dated 30 March 2026 yields about ten thousand ideas with the Talent Acquisition linked filter on the Product Features model.
Aggregate sentiment on the export gauge is modestly negative (about -0.163) with hard effort (about -1.23), signalling systemic workaround pain rather than isolated complaints.
Verbatim row-level text is limited in the materialised scratch dump; quantitative voice leans on capability volume, sentiment, and effort indices from dashboard sheets.

speaker_notes:
• Exact N from 106 report.

References:
• research/India/brainstorm-analysis/2026-04-01-brainstorm-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Communications and Offers combine high N with very hard effort indices, mirroring Teleperformance India pain on notifications and offer documents.
Compliance and Data Privacy sits at 851 ideas with negative sentiment, supporting DPDP-first roadmaps without claiming India-only row counts.
Volumes are global indices; pair with India interviews for regional prioritisation, not standalone proof of India-only prevalence.

speaker_notes:
• Compliance volume from theme validation matrix.

References:
• research/India/brainstorm-analysis/2026-04-01-brainstorm-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Internal ideas ask for requisition-scoped compensation visibility before offer business process steps, echoing India tabular disclosure and early comp visibility pain.
Localisation opt-in by country of work still fails early internal-candidate pay visibility needs according to long-form workaround text in the export.
Candidates struggle to signal intended work location at apply, complicating pay transparency and retention rules across EU, US, and Canada contexts noted in ideation strings.
Bulk screening time-savers appear explicitly, aligning with Teleperformance India seven-hundred-applicant queues and parser accuracy asks.

speaker_notes:
• Quotes are global ideation, not TP verbatims.

References:
• research/India/brainstorm-analysis/2026-04-01-brainstorm-analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 9
Internal SMEs

paragraphs.text: Five Workday experts span product leadership, field readiness, global services, India enterprise architecture (Genpact), and strategic customer engagement (Accenture-scale) from 2024–2025 notes.
Purpose: triangulate fraud, BGC orchestration, India offer disclosure, mass ops, WhatsApp norms, consent models, and DNH or purge automation before customer readout.
Internal views supplement Teleperformance India; slides use SME1–SME5 labels with names only in presenter notes.
Timing spans January 2025 through November 2024 leadership sessions; note fidelity varies from summary notes to richer transcripts per file.

speaker_notes:
• Presenter map: SME1 Bernie; SME2 Fabiola Navarro; SME3 Santosh Gulia; SME4 David Lodola; SME5 David Phillips.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

rows: Product leadership; India FY27; KYC and fraud
Lowe’s volume; India offer and BGC
Genpact talent supply chain; mass hire

speaker_notes:
• Align IDs to 105 participant table.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: KYC and resume fraud discussed at roughly one hundred thousand resumes per month scale with Accenture as a heavy example; banking-grade trust framing is explicit in leadership notes.
FY27 India focus, address localisation, regulations, and partners including interview-intelligence ecosystem signals complement native roadmap depth.
Hypothesis: identity trust is a first-class India pillar with throughput investments, not only services workarounds; P1–P2 echo duplicate urgency at Teleperformance India scale.
Internal brainstorm collateral and enablement contacts were flagged for ongoing India strategy loops in source notes.

speaker_notes:
• Detailed quotes live in internal transcript files listed in 105.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: India offers need tabular, formula-heavy compensation disclosure; two documents to candidates and change cycles track to legal expectations.
Auto-complete hire can skip recruiter start-date confirmation; Extend-style fixes appear when automation conflicts with date truth.
India BGC may hire before all packages complete versus US gating; parallel drug and background multiply reinitiate and integration timing issues.
Customers want one-click BGC reinitiate with vendor signals consumed cleanly inside Job Application business process steps.

speaker_notes:
• Detailed quotes live in internal transcript files listed in 105.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: BGC documents vary by stage but review-document steps misfit pre-interview attach-only needs; candidate-home attachment flexibility with job versus candidate scope recurs in implementations.
Opt-in defaults starve India campaign reach at volume; region-configurable opt-out plus per-email unsubscribe are the fastest fixes in services notes.
WhatsApp dominates candidate links and scheduling appetite; Paradox and broader messaging roadmap dependencies are acknowledged explicitly in services notes.
Fraud at seven-lakh population-scale examples drove ID-before-interview and interviewer confirmation; chatbots for status await compliant conversational channels.

speaker_notes:
• Detailed quotes live in internal transcript files listed in 105.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Genpact used Extend to bridge professional services demand fields into recruiting when standard requisition objects lacked talent supply chain visibility end to end.
Mass hire stories cite one hundred to two hundred concurrent openings with too many individual clicks; bulk offers, agreements, and approvals remain friction points.
Impersonation elevates verification expectations; Tydy middleware plus custom integration appeared where native BGC felt basic versus contractual client obligations.
Weak audit on career site name or phone changes undermines BGC integrity; post-offer no-show drove purchased messaging; HiredScore internal rotation visuals surfaced as limited.

speaker_notes:
• Detailed quotes live in internal transcript files listed in 105.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Accenture-scale asks: do-not-hire, mass purge, merge beyond two duplicates, fraud detection, interview-stage ID validation.
Notes cite roughly two hundred thousand duplicate context and about half of India applications potentially outside Workday; validate per account.
Competitive trickery narratives push AI-assisted detection with human judgement retained; validated professional network concept signals long-term trust bets.
DNH extends beyond ex-employees with analogues for Singapore government and US federal contexts in engagement notes.

speaker_notes:
• Detailed quotes live in internal transcript files listed in 105.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 0
Primary research

paragraphs.text: Five recruiters across Teleperformance India high-volume frontline and specialist or confidential programmes from December 2025 onsite sessions; employer name retained for credibility per research standards.
Participants appear as P1–P5 on slides; quotes are paraphrased from Step 8 findings to balance density with fidelity for VP audiences.
Coverage spans seasonal ramps, agency fees and intake, supervisory organisation gaps, reporting outside Workday, leadership ID policy, and notification noise at thousands of tasks.
Following slides give one participant each with quote-led bullets and jobs-to-be-done anchors from the same research file.

speaker_notes:
• Anonymisation standard: P1–P5 only on slides.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

rows: Frontline hiring lead, North and East

speaker_notes:
• Segment labels from 105 table.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Runs specialised internal recruiting for a compact team; balances day-to-day sourcing and offers with managerial duties such as team attendance that compete with candidate throughput.
Segment is distinct from highest-volume agency-channel hiring yet still depends on the same enterprise approvals, data quality, and Workday hygiene described across the wider TP India programme.
JTBD anchors: keep assigned reqs accurate, move candidates efficiently, and sustain team effectiveness without being pulled entirely into firefighting.

speaker_notes:
• Verbatim strings: see 105 file for exact wording.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Demand and approvals often live in email first; recruiters only raise Workday reqs after long threads, weakening in-system approval history and slowing JR creation.
Recruiters carry cost centre, line of business, and margin detail hiring managers omit, driving rework; one senior hire example lost roughly a day fixing tables before release.
JTBD: accurate reqs with visible approval status and faster, auditable path from demand to posting so recruiters return time to candidate work.

speaker_notes:
• Verbatim strings: see 105 file for exact wording.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Policy requires three government IDs before offer yet candidates can advance without finishing; recruiters chase steps while leadership lacks crisp policy visibility, stretching calendars.
Confidential funnel sees roughly eighty-two to eighty-three percent join but sixteen to seventeen percent loss tied to process length and anxiety, not pay alone, before acceptance.
OTP failures on ID verification drive tickets; hundreds of applications per req force one-by-one review when parsers sit near sixty to seventy percent accuracy.

speaker_notes:
• Verbatim strings: see 105 file for exact wording.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: India agency-channel hiring spans roughly one hundred to one hundred fifty hires per week in peaks with eight hundred to one thousand monthly in span and India-wide eight thousand to nine thousand monthly narrative.
Every application still needs manual duplicate checks under the profile; skipped checks risk agency fee fairness when duplicates are found late.
Vendor upload approval at roughly one hundred profiles per day implies dedicated approvers; regenerate limits after acceptance clashed with early go-live compensation mismatches across large cohorts.

speaker_notes:
• Verbatim strings: see 105 file for exact wording.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Collective peaks near one thousand five hundred to two thousand hires per week across eight to nine sites; July go-live collided with ramp, delaying NTIDs and training starts.
Duplicate signal does not block; agency first-source attribution breaks create financial exposure; Aadhaar-class identifiers are requested for dedupe but are not validation keys today.

Roughly one hundred to one hundred fifty offers per day in span; batch join-date moves cannot be mass-redone in-system, pushing manual legal letters; Saturday maintenance clashes with six-day operations.

speaker_notes:
• Verbatim strings: see 105 file for exact wording.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 1
Thematic analysis

paragraphs.text: Key insight: PAN, Aadhaar, UAN anchor trust; policy needs all IDs pre-offer but UX allows skips; OTP fails and dedupe ignores gov IDs, driving fee risk (P3-P5, SMEs).
Business impact: chase cycles, escalations, and financial exposure when duplicates do not block or honour first-source rules at thousands of profiles per day.
Product implications: lawful gating, UDMF on permitted IDs, partner eKYC or offline XML, fraudulent application detection alignment, DPDP-minimised notices.

speaker_notes:
• Themes from April 2026 India thematic report.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Key insight: peaks near 1500-2000 hires per week and 2000-3000+ agency profiles per day; duplicate review fills days; vendor upload approval bottlenecks (P4, P5, SMEs).
Business impact: FTEs on manual checks, credential delays at go-live, lost business days when bulk tools lag seasonal ramps.
Product implications: duplicate queues, merge beyond two, mass disposition, HiredScore activation for volume, stronger bulk grid for India templates.

speaker_notes:
• Themes from April 2026 India thematic report.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Key insight: offer regeneration limits after pay or date changes force manual legal letters at ~100-150 offers per day; India post-hire BGC needs flexible triggers (P3-P5, SMEs).
Business impact: double-digit offer-to-join loss tied to process length; batch join-date moves hard to redo in-system.
Product implications: controlled regenerate and batch where safe, audit-friendly notifications, BGC flexibility without brittle Studio sprawl.

speaker_notes:
• Themes from April 2026 India thematic report.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Key insight: WhatsApp is default socially; matrix true gaps on +91 SMS and WhatsApp in core UI; customers see thousands of generic task emails missing req context.
Business impact: recruiters phone to confirm tasks; channel parity appears in RFPs versus regional suites and global ATS.
Product implications: native messaging with DPDP consent and logging; richer templates; governed CPaaS interim with Legal.

speaker_notes:
• Themes from April 2026 India thematic report.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Key insight: email approvals precede Workday reqs, causing rework; P2 lost ~24 hours fixing cost centre tables; P3 uses parallel tools when dashboards miss SLA views.
Business impact: weaker approval audit trails, supervisory org mis-tags at scale, slower time-to-post for confidential or volume programmes.
Product implications: in-flow approvals, req ageing for managers, governed questionnaire templates; validate Flowserve presales row with matrix before battle cards.

speaker_notes:
• Themes from April 2026 India thematic report.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

• Matrix transcribed from thematic report Phase 4.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 2
Full funnel

rows: Matrix true gap; P4 parse friction
ID step reliability and BGC orchestration

speaker_notes:
• Funnel labels illustrative; tailor per account workshops.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 3
Roadmap

paragraphs.text: Problem: ID policy demands pre-offer IDs but gating and OTP are weak; P3 and P5 need lawful dedupe on government IDs; SMEs cite impersonation and merge limits at Accenture-style scale.
Recommendation: UDMF plus application gating for PAN, Aadhaar, UAN where permitted; DPDP consent; partner eKYC or offline XML; recruiter exceptions; align fraudulent application detection.
Why now: new market entry and trust buying; RICE about 1052, reach 4500, composite 2.75, confidence 85 percent, effort 10 person-months.
Success: shorter ID chase loops on pilots; aim for roughly five to ten percent time-to-hire gain where measured against India high-volume baselines.

speaker_notes:
• Legal review on purge and Aadhaar-adjacent flows.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Problem and evidence: thousands of profiles per day need duplicate review; agency first-source rules break fees; vendor upload approval does not scale; P4 and P5 plus SMEs cite merge capped at two records.
Recommendation: productise duplicate queues, first-source and agency attribution, multi-way merge, mass disposition with compliance guardrails, and faster vendor-upload UX for daily throughput.
Why now: India scale growth row plus core ATS parity; score about 1238 with reach 4000, composite 2.75, confidence 90 percent, effort 8 person-months.
Success: cut recruiter time in manual duplicate review on pilot cohorts; stabilise or improve JR load per recruiter while hire volume rises.

speaker_notes:
• Legal review on purge and Aadhaar-adjacent flows.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Problem: 700+ applications per req exhaust manual screening; parser limits force eyeball review; Q2 Priority 2 needs AI matching ROI on high-volume India IT and BPO templates.
Recommendation: repeatable HiredScore activation with reviewer queues, governance for EU AI Act and India AI expectations, and tenant readiness gates before broad rollout.
Why now: explicit quarterly AI matching betas; score about 990 with reach 2500, composite 2.75, confidence 72 percent, effort 5 person-months.
Success: shorten shortlist time on pilot reqs versus control; pair with adoption percent of recruiters using ranked views.

speaker_notes:
• Legal review on purge and Aadhaar-adjacent flows.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Problem and evidence: matrix true gaps on native +91 SMS and WhatsApp in core Recruiting UI; SMEs and PESTEL Social show WhatsApp as default; customers suffer task-email noise without richer channel choice.
Recommendation: deliver native candidate messaging for India-first GTM with DPDP consent, logging, opt-out, and template governance; keep interim CPaaS patterns documented until parity lands.
Why now: India scale row plus next-wave roadmap references; score about 804 with reach 6000, composite 2.50, confidence 75 percent, effort 14 person-months.
Success: lift channel adoption among recruiters on pilots; pair with candidate funnel proxies where instrumentation exists.

speaker_notes:
• Legal review on purge and Aadhaar-adjacent flows.

References:
• research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-04-01-India-PMF-Analysis-INDIA-PMF-006.md

paragraphs.text: Problem and evidence: compensation or start-date shifts after acceptance force manual legal letters at batch scale; P3 to P5 cite regenerate friction alongside ID-policy delays that compound offer-to-join risk.
Recommendation: expand controlled offer and employment agreement regeneration, cautious batch updates where risk allows, and candidate notifications that preserve audit trails for Legal review.
Why now: core ATS parity plus India operating reality; score about 921 with reach 3500, composite 2.25, confidence 82 percent, effort 7 person-months.
Success: reduce renegotiation loops contributing to delay; measure time-to-hire segments before and after on governed pilots.

speaker_notes:
• Legal review on purge and Aadhaar-adjacent flows.
